description: CAPT-GOLDEN — a request to change a TOLERANCE BAND or a steering AIM, raised against a Discrepancy, and RESOLVED by an EXTERNAL authority (not the engine). This is the Golden counterpart to SMProfile-ResolutionProposal, and the two are deliberately different kinds of object. A ResolutionProposal is symmetric evidence closed INSIDE the system (fix_reality acts on the machine; fix_charter becomes an osf-schemas PR whose merge IS the approval). A ChangeRequest is narrower and asymmetric: it asks specifically 'move the band to [lo,hi]' or 'move the aim', and it can only be CLOSED by an authority that owns the number being challenged — a drawing/customer_spec/norm band is NOT the engine's to change, so the engine records the request and carries the authority's ruling VERBATIM. The band the authority hands back (`authoritative_band`) is copied in unaltered at resolution, next to who ruled it (`resolved_by`, an external role) and a signature over the ruling (`resolution_signature`) — so 'the band is now [x,y] because Authority Z ruled it on date D, signed S' is auditable end to end. `tolerance_source` is the SAME vocabulary as recipe-schema.json toleranceSource (drawing / customer_spec / norm / process_estimate / unknown), because WHO may resolve the request follows from WHERE the number came from: a process_estimate can be re-set by Process Engineering; a drawing/customer_spec/norm band needs the drawing/customer/standards owner. INVARIANTS the engine enforces (NOT expressible in this schema): (1) a ChangeRequest MUST carry at least one of `proposed_band` / `proposed_aim`; (2) on status resolved_approved the matching `authoritative_band`/`authoritative_aim` + `resolved_by` + `resolved_at` + `resolution_signature` MUST be present; (3) `resolved_by` MUST be an external authority, never the `requested_by`. The store owner is the discrepancy-engine (system of record); the KG node is written straight in — not edge-sourced — so it carries NO delivery/scope/promotion wire-contract.

attributes.name: change_request_id
attributes.description: Unique change-request id (uuid). This is the CR's own identity ('id' in the Golden brief); kept as change_request_id so it matches the <entity>_id convention every other intelligence node uses (discrepancy_id, proposal_id, rule_id) and is unambiguous as the kgIdProperty.

attributes.name: discrepancy_id
attributes.description: The Discrepancy this change-request answers — the divergence that motivated challenging the band/aim. Also drawn as the ABOUT_DISCREPANCY edge.

attributes.name: machine_id
attributes.description: The machine the band/aim governs (the process the request is about). Also drawn as the ABOUT_MACHINE edge.

attributes.name: constraint_id
attributes.description: The band/constraint being challenged — the recipe band ref or profile constraint id (e.g. 'recipe:part_mass_band' / 'recipe_part_mass_band' / 'spindle_temp_control_limits'). An attribute, not an edge: constraints live inside recipes/profiles, there is no Constraint KG node to point at.

attributes.name: tolerance_source
attributes.description: Provenance of the band being challenged — the SAME vocabulary as recipe-schema.json toleranceSource. It decides WHO can resolve: drawing / customer_spec / norm are BINDING (only the drawing/customer/standards owner may change them); process_estimate is optimisable (Process Engineering); unknown means the origin was never recorded and the request is ungrounded until it is bound.

attributes.name: proposed_band
attributes.description: The proposed NEW tolerance band, serialized JSON [lo,hi] (same shape as recipe `values` bands). One of proposed_band / proposed_aim MUST be set (engine-enforced). Serialized-JSON String, matching the house style for structured intelligence fields (cf. ResolutionProposal.constraint_diff / evidence_refs).

attributes.name: proposed_aim
attributes.description: The proposed NEW steering aim (recipe parameters[].aim) — where the process should be centred inside the band. One of proposed_band / proposed_aim MUST be set (engine-enforced). Moving the aim never moves soll or the band; it is a steering change, which is why it can often be resolved without the drawing owner.

attributes.name: rationale
attributes.description: Why the change is requested — the evidence the authority rules on (e.g. Cp/Ca measured over the current regime, recurrence count, the drawing/spec the current band contradicts).

attributes.name: requested_by
attributes.description: Who raised the request: steward/user id, rule_id or agent id. MUST differ from resolved_by (engine-enforced).

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: open -> resolved_approved | resolved_rejected. resolved_approved means the authority granted a band/aim (see authoritative_*); resolved_rejected means the current band/aim stands (see resolution_note). Terminal states are both signed (resolution_signature).

attributes.name: authoritative_band
attributes.description: The band the EXTERNAL authority ruled, copied in VERBATIM at resolution, serialized JSON [lo,hi]. This — not proposed_band — is the band that becomes truth; the proposal is only the ask. Set on resolved_approved when a band was granted.

attributes.name: authoritative_aim
attributes.description: The steering aim the authority ruled, verbatim at resolution. Set on resolved_approved when an aim was granted (symmetric to proposed_aim).

attributes.name: resolved_by
attributes.description: The EXTERNAL authority that resolved the request — the owner of the number's provenance (drawing/customer/standards owner for binding bands, Process Engineering for process_estimate). MUST NOT be requested_by (four-eyes, engine-enforced). The engine builds NO governance of its own here; it records the authority's decision.

attributes.name: resolution_note
attributes.description: The authority's rationale for the decision — especially required for resolved_rejected (why the band/aim stands).

attributes.name: resolution_signature
attributes.description: Signature over the resolution (Ed25519 / vault-hashchain ref) — the verbatim-authority proof that ties authoritative_band/authoritative_aim + resolved_by + resolved_at together into an auditable ruling. Same hash-chain the Discrepancy lifecycle events use.

attributes.name: audit_ref
attributes.description: Hash-chain ref (vault-hashchain) for the change-request events — same Ed25519 chain as the Discrepancy lifecycle and ResolutionProposal events.

relationships.description: The machine whose band/aim the request concerns.
